preamble: The transformation from a voiceless aspirated retroflex affricate (ʈʂʰ) to a voiceless postalveolar fricative (ʃ) represents a common lenition process observed in various Sinitic languages. This shift involves a reduction in articulatory complexity, moving from a stop-fricative sequence with aspiration and a retroflex place of articulation to a simpler fricative with a postalveolar place and no aspiration.
phoneticEffects: De-affrication (affricate to fricative), loss of aspiration, and a reduction in retroflexion to a postalveolar place of articulation

examples.note: This shift is observed for pinyin 'ch-' initials in Taiwanese Mandarin, influenced by Hokkien, and is also found in other Mandarin dialects across southern China.
